title: Hemionitis ornithopus
intro: Hemionitis ornithopus, also known as the ferny liverwort, is a species of liverwort that belongs to the class Marchantiophyta, a group of non-vascular, leafy plants. The health benefits of Hemionitis ornithopus include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe and protect the skin, as well as support the immune system. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Hemionitis ornithopus is commonly used in traditional medicine and is often prepared as a tea or infused oil, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves to harness its potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Hemionitis ornithopus is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This classification places it within the larger group of leptosporangiate ferns. The family Dryopteridaceae is a diverse group of ferns commonly known as the wood fern family. The genus Hemionitis is a small group of ferns found in tropical and subtropical regions. Hemionitis ornithopus is native to the Old World and can be found in a variety of habitats, including forests and woodlands.
